binding.value:
<script type='text/javascript'>
 if (parent!=window){ //Is inside frame
  document.getElementById('" + lnkPopup.Id + "').style.display='';
  document.getElementById('" + lnkNormal.Id + "').style.display='none';
 } 
 else {
  document.getElementById('" + lnkPopup.Id + "').style.display='none';
  document.getElementById('" + lnkNormal.Id + "').style.display='';
 }
</script>

children.text: This screen shows which addresses are forbidden to login (they were blocked due to consecutive failed login attempts). Unblocking these addresses enables users to login again from that IP address.

children.emptyMessage: "There are currently no blocked IP addresses"

name: FAQ
label: FAQ
description: Frequent asked questions screen

children.text: Q: Can I use this component to integrate with other federated identity Provider?
A: Yes. Although it was tested with Tested with Okta, Azure, ADFS, PingOne and OneLogin, this is a generic connector for SAML integrations. Field names may vary, but the logic should be the same.
Q: What if I want to customize the login user data synchronization (e.g.: automatically grant roles, create user photo, etc).
A: The logic for automatic user provisioning and association to groups is implemented in the User_Check user action, which is executed in the callback (Preparation of the IdP screen).
Q: How can I implement a federated authentication with SAML protocol in my mobile applications?
component to allow your OutSystems mobile apps to use the IdP connector component to authenticate and authorize a user, through the InAppBrowserEvents plugin.
